title: Team Subagent Orchestrator
description: Enable a leader main agent to manage 3-5 subagents for long-running multi-iteration tasks. Leader focuses on thinking, planning, delegation, tracking, and course correction — not direct execution.

input_schema.goal: string — end-to-end objective and success criteria
input_schema.constraints: string — time budget, quality bar, scope boundaries, available tools
input_schema.available_agents: string[] — candidate agents with capability descriptions
input_schema.milestones: string[] — checkpoint definitions and expected deliverables per phase
input_schema.iteration_budget: number — maximum iterations before forced convergence

role_charter_template.depends_on: string — other roles or external inputs
role_charter_template.must_not: string — out-of-scope actions to prevent overlap

heartbeat_template.current_action: string — what I am doing right now
heartbeat_template.sub_progress: string — X of Y done or percentage

agent_finished_log_template.lesson: string — optional, fill if task taught something reusable

persistence_rules: Every agent writes state at end of each cycle.
Every agent reads persisted state at start of each cycle.
Leader verifies all agents restored state before dispatching.
On state loss, leader reconstructs from task board + finished logs + lessons ledger.
Use structured formats (tables, JSON) over prose for persisted state.
leader_decision_priority: Correct — intervene on drifting workstreams with specific guidance.
Advance — push next highest-priority work item.
Refine — improve plan quality only when all above are clear.
leader_anti_patterns: Doing execution work directly instead of delegating.
Giving vague instructions like 'make it better'.
Changing subagent roles mid-iteration without re-charter.
Moving to next iteration before milestone gate passes.
Skipping retrospective — lessons not recorded are lessons lost.
Not reading the lessons ledger before planning — repeating past mistakes.
Letting agents skip todo updates — invisible work creates invisible risk.
Allowing subagents to go silent during long execution — require heartbeats.
Issuing adjustments without concrete trigger — anxiety is not a reason.
Sending a stream of small adjustments — batch when possible.

failure_recovery.response: force convergence on critical path only
probe immediately; if no response, reassign task

context_management: Summarize completed work as compact artifacts, not raw conversation.
Pass only relevant context per dispatch — not entire history.
Use task board as single source of truth.
Archive completed workstreams, carry forward only residual items.
Persist lessons ledger separately — do not inline into dispatch messages.
instructions: BOOTSTRAP: Decompose goal into 3-5 parallel workstreams with clear boundaries.
BOOTSTRAP: Assign one subagent per workstream using the role charter template.
BOOTSTRAP: Initialize shared task board, all agent todo/done logs, and empty lessons ledger.
ITERATE/PLAN: Leader reviews task board and assigns work units with scope, inputs, done criteria, and deadline signal.
ITERATE/DELEGATE: Dispatch instructions telling each subagent exactly what to do, what NOT to do, and when to report back.
ITERATE/EXECUTE: Subagents work within role boundary, send heartbeats at specified intervals, respond to probes, and accept dynamic adjustments.
ITERATE/COLLECT: Leader gathers status reports, reviews heartbeats, probes silent or drifting agents, and acts on low-confidence or blocked items immediately.
ITERATE/INTEGRATE: Leader merges outputs, resolves conflicts, updates task board.
ITERATE/CORRECT: Leader evaluates drift and decides: continue, rebalance, rework, re-scope, or issue dynamic adjustments to in-flight subagents.
ITERATE/RETRO: Collect per-cycle retro from each subagent, update lessons ledger, adjust next plan.
ITERATE/PERSIST: All agents write current todo, done log, and retro state to persistent storage.
CONVERGE: Run final quality checks, collect self-assessments, fix integration gaps. No new scope.
CLOSE: Produce integrated output, write project-level retrospective, document residual risks, write next-iteration backlog, archive all state (task board, agent logs, lessons ledger).

examples.input: Migrate monolith to microservices across 6 iterations.
examples.output: Leader assigns 4 subagents: Architect (service boundaries, API contracts), Builder (migration per service), Tester (integration tests, benchmarks), Ops (pipeline, canary). Iteration 1: Architect defines contracts, Tester prepares harness, Ops sets up staging. Iteration 3 correction: Tester reports coverage gap on payment service → Leader pauses Builder's next module, redirects to payment tests first.

examples.input: Rewrite all user-facing docs for product rebrand.
examples.output: Leader assigns 3 subagents: Writer (draft per section), Reviewer (accuracy & style), Asset Creator (diagrams, screenshots). Leader prioritizes top-traffic pages, gates each batch before publish, runs parallel draft + asset creation.

examples.input: Diagnose and fix an intermittent production crash.
examples.output: Leader assigns 3 subagents: Log Analyst (pattern identification), Code Inspector (trace suspicious paths), Reproducer (build minimal repro). Leader manages hypothesis list, dispatches parallel evidence gathering, eliminates hypotheses per cycle, narrows until root cause confirmed.
